schemas.name: Trademark Renewal — 10-Year Extension
schemas.description: Trademark renewal for 10-year extension. TM-R filing. Expired trademark restoration. Protect your brand continuity. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: Trademark Renewal
schemas.description: Trademark renewal for 10-year extension. TM-R filing. Expired trademark restoration. Protect your brand continuity. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: Trademark Renewal
schemas.description: Trademark renewal for 10-year extension. TM-R filing. Expired trademark restoration. Protect your brand continuity. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Check expiry date on registration certificate
step.name: Step 1

step.text: File TM-R on IP India portal before expiry
step.name: Step 2

acceptedAnswer.text: File TM-R 6 months before the expiry date (10 years from registration/last renewal). Early filing recommended to avoid last-minute issues.

mainEntity.name: Can an expired trademark be restored?

acceptedAnswer.text: Within 6 months of expiry: yes, with surcharge. 6-12 months: at Registrar's discretion. Beyond 12 months: mark is cancelled and must be re-applied.

mainEntity.name: When does a trademark need renewal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Every 10 years from the date of registration. Renewal application can be filed 6 months before expiry. If missed, 6-month grace period with additional fee. After grace period: restoration within 1 year (with higher fees).

mainEntity.name: How much does trademark renewal cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government fee: ₹9,000 per class (timely renewal). Late renewal (surcharge): additional ₹4,500. Restoration fee (after expiry): ₹9,000 + restoration charges. Professional fee: ₹2,000-₹5,000.

mainEntity.name: What happens if a trademark is not renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Trademark is removed from the Register after expiry + 6-month grace period. Can be restored within 1 year of removal. After 1 year: mark is dead and anyone can apply for it. You lose ® protection.

mainEntity.name: Can a trademark be renewed indefinitely?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Trademark registration can be renewed every 10 years indefinitely, as long as renewal fees are paid. Some of the oldest trademarks in the world are 100+ years old and still renewed.

mainEntity.name: Is the trademark renewal process online?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. File TM-R form online through the IP India portal. Attach proof of use and pay renewal fees. No examination or publication required for renewal. Processing time: 2-4 weeks.
